what: Download and Process Future Climate Variables from CHELSA v2.1 (CMIP6) — get_chelsa_future
title: Download and Process Future Climate Variables from CHELSA v2.1 (CMIP6) — get_chelsa_future
text: Downloads future bioclimatic variables CHELSA v2.1 CMIP6 climate scenarios. Data available three SSP scenarios, five GCMs (ISIMIP3b selection), three future periods. Files served Cloud Optimized GeoTIFFs (COGs) Swiss WSL EnviCloud, enabling efficient spatial subsetting via /vsicurl/ without downloading global files. One bioclimatic variables (bio1–bio19) can requested single call. result multi-layer SpatRaster optionally cropped masked AOI, consistent interface get_worldclim_future.

title: Download and Process Future Climate Variables from CHELSA v2.1 (CMIP6) — get_chelsa_future
code: get_chelsa_future(   var = "bio1",   scenario = "ssp585",   period = "2041-2070",   gcm = "MPI-ESM1-2-HR",   aoi = NULL,   destination_dir = NULL,   timeout = 300 )

title: Download and Process Future Climate Variables from CHELSA v2.1 (CMIP6) — get_chelsa_future
text: var `character` vector. One CHELSA bioclimatic variable names ("bio1" "bio19"), "" download 19. Default: "bio1". scenario `character`. SSP emission scenario. Options: "ssp126" — SSP1-2.6 (low emissions, sustainable development). "ssp370" — SSP3-7.0 (high emissions, regional rivalry). "ssp585" — SSP5-8.5 (high emissions, fossil-fueled growth). Default: "ssp585". period `character`. Future climatological period. Options: "2011-2040" — Near future. "2041-2070" — Mid future. "2071-2100" — Far future. Default: "2041-2070". gcm `character`. Global Circulation Model following ISIMIP3b selection. Options: "GFDL-ESM4"    — Priority 1 (highest priority). "IPSL-CM6A-LR" — Priority 2. "MPI-ESM1-2-HR" — Priority 3. "MRI-ESM2-0"   — Priority 4. "UKESM1-0-LL"  — Priority 5. fewer five models used, selection follow priority order. Default: "MPI-ESM1-2-HR". aoi `sf` `SpatVector` `NULL`. Area interest used crop mask raster. NULL (default), global raster returned. Providing AOI strongly recommended given file sizes. destination_dir `character` `NULL`. Directory output .tif saved. NULL, temporary directory used. timeout `numeric`. Maximum time seconds per HTTP request. Default: 300.

title: Download and Process Future Climate Variables from CHELSA v2.1 (CMIP6) — get_chelsa_future
text: SpatRaster one layer per requested variable.   aoi provided, raster cropped masked . Also   written destination_dir single multi-layer .tif.   Returns NULL invisibly error.

title: Download and Process Future Climate Variables from CHELSA v2.1 (CMIP6) — get_chelsa_future
text: ## GCM availability CHELSA v2.1 future projections follow ISIMIP3b model selection, provides five GCMs covering range climate sensitivities regional performance. SSP × GCM × period combinations guaranteed available server. combination unavailable, function emits warning returns NULL variable. ## SSP scenarios available CHELSA v2.1 provides SSP126, SSP370, SSP585. Note SSP245 (available WorldClim) **available** CHELSA v2.1 future bioclimatic variables. ## COG streaming Files use /vsicurl/ GDAL virtual filesystem stream tiles covering aoi remote COG, avoiding full file downloads. ## Comparison get_worldclim_future() CHELSA uses mechanistic downscaling; WorldClim uses statistical interpolation. CHELSA offers SSP126/370/585; WorldClim offers SSP126/245/370/585. CHELSA provides 5 GCMs (ISIMIP3b); WorldClim provides 23 GCMs. ~1 km (30 arc-second) resolution. regions complex terrain, CHELSA generally preferred.

title: Download and Process Future Climate Variables from CHELSA v2.1 (CMIP6) — get_chelsa_future
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(sf)  # Use Costa Rica outline (included in paisaje) aoi <- paisaje::cr_outline_c  # Single variable — Annual mean temperature, mid-century, pessimistic bio1_fut <- get_chelsa_future(   var      = "bio1",   scenario = "ssp585",   period   = "2041-2070",   gcm      = "MPI-ESM1-2-HR",   aoi      = aoi )  # Multiple variables — near future, optimistic bio_stack <- get_chelsa_future(   var      = c("bio1", "bio12", "bio15"),   scenario = "ssp126",   period   = "2011-2040",   gcm      = "GFDL-ESM4",   aoi      = aoi )  # Compare historic vs future bio1 for Costa Rica bio1_hist <- get_chelsa_historic(var = "bio1", aoi = aoi) bio1_diff <- bio1_fut - bio1_hist terra::plot(bio1_diff, main = "Temperature change (bio1): 2041-2070 SSP585")  # Extract per H3 hexagon h7      <- paisaje::get_h3_grid(aoi, res = 7) h7_fut  <- extract_num_raster(bio_stack, h7) } # }

title: Download and Process Historic Climate Variables from CHELSA v2.1 — get_chelsa_historic
text: ## CHELSA WorldClim? CHELSA v2.1 WorldClim v2.1 high-resolution (~1 km) global climatologies, differ downscaling methodology: CHELSA uses **mechanistic downscaling** approach based         atmospheric dynamics orographic effects, tends perform         better complex terrain (mountains, coasts). WorldClim uses **statistical interpolation** (thin-plate splines),         faster less physically grounded. tropical regions complex topography (e.g., Costa Rica),         CHELSA generally considered accurate. ## COG streaming — full download required CHELSA files Cloud Optimized GeoTIFFs hosted Swiss WSL EnviCloud. function uses /vsicurl/ virtual filesystem prefix GDAL (via terra) stream tiles cover aoi, avoiding downloading entire global file. aoi provided, spatial subsetting done memory writing disk. ## Reference period historic CHELSA v2.1 bioclimatic variables use **1981–2010** climatological normal period. future projections see get_chelsa_future.

what: Generate an H3 Hexagonal Grid for an sf Object — get_h3_grid
title: Generate an H3 Hexagonal Grid for an sf Object — get_h3_grid
text: Generates hexagonal grid H3 cells specified resolution intersect given `sf` object. wrapper functions h3jsr package.

what: Download and Retrieve Nightlight Data from NASA Black Marble — get_nightlight_data
title: Download and Retrieve Nightlight Data from NASA Black Marble — get_nightlight_data
text: Downloads NASA Black Marble nighttime lights raster data given area interest (aoi_sf), time period, temporal resolution (daily, monthly, annual). Internally wraps bm_raster blackmarbler package (World Bank), handles tile discovery, download, mosaicing, cloud/quality masking automatically. result SpatRaster cropped masked AOI, ready passed directly extract_num_raster terra-based workflow paisaje.

title: Download and Retrieve Nightlight Data from NASA Black Marble — get_nightlight_data
code: get_nightlight_data(   aoi_sf,   year,   month = NULL,   product_id = "VNP46A3",   bearer,   variable = NULL,   quality_flag_rm = NULL,   destination_dir = NULL,   timeout = 1200 )

title: Download and Retrieve Nightlight Data from NASA Black Marble — get_nightlight_data
text: aoi_sf `sf`. sf object defining area interest (AOI). Can polygon geometry (country outline, H3 grid extent, custom boundary). raster cropped masked extent. year `numeric` `character`. year interest (e.g., 2022). Used together month build date argument passed bm_raster. month `numeric` NULL. Month year (1–12). Required product_id = "VNP46A3" (monthly composite). Ignored annual products ("VNP46A4"). Default NULL. product_id `character`. NASA Black Marble product identifier. Available options: "VNP46A1" — Daily, -sensor radiance (500 m). "VNP46A2" — Daily, BRDF-corrected gap-filled (500 m).     Default variable: Gap_Filled_DNB_BRDF-Corrected_NTL. "VNP46A3" — Monthly composite, snow-free (500 m).     Default variable: NearNadir_Composite_Snow_Free. "VNP46A4" — Annual composite, snow-free (500 m).     Default variable: NearNadir_Composite_Snow_Free. Default: "VNP46A3" (monthly). bearer `character`. NASA LAADS DAAC bearer token required authentication. Obtain free token https://ladsweb.modaps.eosdis.nasa.gov/ Login > Generate Token. strongly recommended store token environment variable (e.g., NASA_BEARER) retrieve Sys.getenv("NASA_BEARER") rather hardcoding scripts. variable `character` NULL. Specific variable (layer) extract HDF5 product. NULL (default), package default product_id used (see parameter description ). Pass "" trigger informative error listing valid variable names. quality_flag_rm `integer vector` NULL. Quality flag values pixels set NA. Lower quality values can removed reduce noise. Default NULL (quality filtering). destination_dir `character` NULL. Directory output .tif intermediate HDF5 tiles cached. NULL (default), system's temporary directory (tempdir) used message emitted. timeout `numeric`. Maximum time seconds allowed HTTP downloads. Temporarily overrides getOption("timeout") restores original value exit. Default: 1200 (20 minutes).

title: Download and Retrieve Nightlight Data from NASA Black Marble — get_nightlight_data
text: SpatRaster object cropped masked aoi_sf,   containing requested nighttime lights variable. Layer name reflects   product date. Also written destination_dir .tif.   Returns NULL invisibly error occurs, informative message.

title: Download and Retrieve Nightlight Data from NASA Black Marble — get_nightlight_data
text: ## NASA Black Marble EOG? previous implementation downloaded global monthly raster (~500 MB) Earth Observation Group (EOG, Colorado School Mines) via HTML scraping, regardless AOI size. NASA Black Marble improves several ways: AOI-aware: downloads MODIS/VIIRS tiles intersect         aoi_sf, dramatically reducing download size regional studies. Higher scientific quality: applies lunar irradiance modeling,         atmospheric correction, BRDF correction, cloud masking         algorithm level (post-hoc). Daily, monthly, annual products unified interface. Stable API: accesses NASA LAADS DAAC via token-authenticated         httr2 requests — fragile HTML scraping. Resolution: 500 m (vs. ~750 m EOG monthly_notile product). ## Bearer token setup NASA bearer token free required. Recommended setup: ## Integration paisaje returned SpatRaster ready passed directly extract_num_raster summarize nightlight values per polygon H3 hexagon grid.
code: # In .Renviron (open with usethis::edit_r_environ()): NASA_BEARER=your_token_here  # Then in your script: bearer <- Sys.getenv("NASA_BEARER")

title: Download and Retrieve Nightlight Data from NASA Black Marble — get_nightlight_data

(2024). blackmarbler: Georeferenced Rasters Statistics Nighttime Lights NASA Black Marble. R package v0.2.5. World Bank. https://worldbank.github.io/blackmarbler/

title: Download and Retrieve Nightlight Data from NASA Black Marble — get_nightlight_data
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(sf)  # Use Costa Rica outline (included in paisaje) aoi <- paisaje::cr_outline_c  # Bearer token from environment variable (recommended) bearer <- Sys.getenv("NASA_BEARER")  # Monthly composite — March 2022 ntl <- get_nightlight_data(   aoi_sf     = aoi,   year       = 2022,   month      = 3,   product_id = "VNP46A3",   bearer     = bearer )  # Annual composite — 2021 ntl_anual <- get_nightlight_data(   aoi_sf     = aoi,   year       = 2021,   product_id = "VNP46A4",   bearer     = bearer )  # Extract mean nightlight per H3 hexagon h7     <- paisaje::get_h3_grid(aoi, res = 7) h7_ntl <- extract_num_raster(ntl, h7) } # }

title: Download and process future environmental variables from WorldClim v2.1 — get_worldclim_future
text: Downloads future climate data WorldClim based CMIP6 climate models SSP scenarios. data can retrieved various spatial resolutions optionally clipped specified area interest (AOI).

code: get_worldclim_future(   var = "bioc",   res = "30s",   scenario = "585",   time_range = "2021-2040",   gcm = "ACCESS-CM2",   aoi = NULL,   retries = 3,   timeout = 300,   destination_dir = NULL )

title: Download and process future environmental variables from WorldClim v2.1 — get_worldclim_future
text: var character Climate variable download. Options: "bioc" — Bioclimatic variables (19 variables) "prec" — Precipitation "tavg" — Average temperature "tmin" — Minimum temperature "tmax" — Maximum temperature Default "bioc". res character Spatial resolution data. Options: "30s" — ~1 km (30 arc-seconds) "2.5m" — ~5 km (2.5 arc-minutes) "5m" — ~10 km (5 arc-minutes) "10m" — ~20 km (10 arc-minutes) Default "30s". scenario character SSP scenario. Options: "126" — SSP1-2.6 (low emissions) "245" — SSP2-4.5 (intermediate emissions) "370" — SSP3-7.0 (high emissions) "585" — SSP5-8.5 (high emissions) Default "585". time_range character Time period. Options: "2021-2040" "2041-2060" "2061-2080" "2081-2100" Default "2021-2040". gcm character General Circulation Model. Options: "ACCESS-CM2", "ACCESS-ESM1-5", "AWI-CM-1-1-MR", "BCC-CSM2-MR", "CanESM5", "CanESM5-CanOE", "CMCC-ESM2", "CNRM-CM6-1", "CNRM-CM6-1-HR", "CNRM-ESM2-1", "EC-Earth3-Veg", "EC-Earth3-Veg-LR", "FIO-ESM-2-0", "GFDL-ESM4", "GISS-E2-1-G", "GISS-E2-1-H", "HadGEM3-GC31-LL", "INM-CM4-8", "INM-CM5-0", "IPSL-CM6A-LR", "MIROC-ES2L", "MIROC6", "MPI-ESM1-2-HR", "MPI-ESM1-2-LR", "MRI-ESM2-0", "UKESM1-0-LL". Default "ACCESS-CM2". aoi sf SpatRaster Optional area interest clip data. Default NULL (clipping). retries integer Number attempts retry download case failure. Default 3. timeout numeric Download timeout seconds. Default 300. destination_dir character Directory downloaded data stored. Default NULL (uses temporary directory).

text: var Character. Variable climática descargar. Opciones: "bio" — Variables bioclimáticas. "tavg" — Temperatura media. "tmin" — Temperatura mínima. "tmax" — Temperatura máxima. "prec" — Precipitación. "srad" — Radiación solar. "wind" — Velocidad del viento. "vapr" — Presión de vapor. Por defecto: `"bio"`. res Numeric. Resolución espacial en minutos de arco. Valores válidos: `0.5`, `2.5`, `5`, `10`. Estos valores se mapean internamente cadenas aceptadas por WorldClim: 0.5 → "30s" 2.5 → "2.5m" 5   → "5m" 10  → "10m" Por defecto: `10`. aoi sf o SpatRaster opcional. Área de interés para recortar los datos. retries Integer. Número de intentos de descarga en caso de fallo. Por defecto: `3`. timeout Numeric. Tiempo máximo de descarga en segundos. Por defecto: `300`. destination_dir Character. Carpeta donde guardar los datos descargados. Si NULL, se usa un directorio temporal.

what: paisaje: Spatial and Environmental Data Tools for Landscape Ecology — paisaje-package
title: paisaje: Spatial and Environmental Data Tools for Landscape Ecology — paisaje-package
text: Provides functions landscape analysis data retrieval. package allows users download environmental variables global datasets (e.g., WorldClim, CHELSA, ESA WorldCover, Nighttime Lights), compute spatial landscape metrics using hexagonal grid system based H3 spatial index. useful ecological modeling, biodiversity studies, spatial data processing landscape ecology. title: paisaje 0.3.0
text: get_chelsa_historic() — Downloads CHELSA v2.1 bioclimatic variables (bio1–bio19) 1981–2010 reference period. Uses Cloud Optimized GeoTIFF (COG) streaming via /vsicurl/, spatial subset covering AOI retrieved — full global download required. Accepts var = "" request 19 variables single call. get_chelsa_future() — Downloads CHELSA v2.1 future bioclimatic variables CMIP6 scenarios (SSP126, SSP370, SSP585) three periods (2011–2040, 2041–2070, 2071–2100) five ISIMIP3b GCMs (GFDL-ESM4, IPSL-CM6A-LR, MPI-ESM1-2-HR, MRI-ESM2-0, UKESM1-0-LL). Also uses COG streaming. Note: SSP245 available CHELSA v2.1.

title: paisaje 0.3.0
text: get_nightlight_data() — Complete rewrite. function now wraps blackmarbler::bm_raster() (World Bank) instead scraping EOG (Earth Observation Group) website. Interface change: new required aoi_sf argument (sf object) replaces year/month first argument, bearer argument (NASA LAADS DAAC token) now required. function returns SpatRaster cropped masked AOI instead file path. See ?get_nightlight_data details obtaining bearer token.

title: paisaje 0.3.0
text: get_nightlight_data() now supports daily (VNP46A1, VNP46A2), monthly (VNP46A3), annual (VNP46A4) NASA Black Marble products 500 m resolution, built-quality filtering via quality_flag_rm. Migrated magrittr pipe (%>%) native R pipe (|>) throughout package (extract_cat_raster(), get_records_by_hexagon()). Requires R >= 4.1.0 (already declared Depends).

title: paisaje 0.3.0
text: Added: blackmarbler (replaces EOG scraping nightlight data). Removed: rvest (longer needed EOG scraper dropped). Removed: magrittr (replaced native pipe |>).

title: paisaje 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-01-07 First CRAN release (2026-01-07). Functions downloading environmental variables: get_worldclim_historic(), get_worldclim_future(), get_esa_10m(), get_nightlight_data(). Hexagonal grid tools: get_h3_grid(), get_records_by_hexagon(). Zonal statistics: extract_num_raster(), extract_cat_raster(). Landscape metrics: calculate_it_metrics(). Biodiversity data: get_records(), count_points_in_polygons(). Included dataset: cr_outline_c (Costa Rica continental outline).
